title: Lasiocephalus mojandensis
taxonomy: Lasiocephalus mojandensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Lasiocephalus, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America. The plant is further classified under the order Asterales, which includes other flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers. The classification of Lasiocephalus mojandensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Lasiocephalus, Species: L. mojandensis. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Lasiocephalus mojandensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Mojanda daisy or Andean daisy. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasiocephalus mojandensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including colds, flu, and skin irritations.
